name: bun-development
description: Modern JavaScript/TypeScript development with Bun runtime. Covers package management, bundling, testing, and migration from Node.js. Use when working with Bun, optimizing JS/TS development speed, or migrating from Node.js to Bun.
capabilities: Install and configure Bun runtime on macOS, Linux, and Windows
Create and initialize new Bun projects with TypeScript support
Manage packages using Bun's built-in package manager
Run and test JavaScript/TypeScript code with Bun
Build and bundle applications for production
Use Bun's built-in APIs for HTTP, SQLite, and file operations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a legitimate Bun runtime development skill. All 117 static findings are false positives: external commands are standard Bun CLI tools, network URLs point to official Bun documentation, environment access patterns are standard Bun/Node.js development practices, and the pipe-to-shell pattern is the official Bun installation method. No malicious intent detected.
valueStatement: This skill provides comprehensive documentation for developing with the Bun JavaScript runtime, covering installation, project setup, package management, testing, and migration from Node.js.
limitations: Does not support all Node.js APIs that rely on older JavaScript runtimes
Some npm packages may have compatibility issues with Bun
Windows support is newer and less mature than macOS/Linux
Limited enterprise tooling compared to Node.js ecosystem

useCases.targetUser: Developers starting new projects wanting faster tooling
useCases.title: New Project Initialization
useCases.description: Quickly set up new JavaScript/TypeScript projects with Bun's fast package manager and built-in tooling

useCases.targetUser: Teams looking to optimize existing Node.js applications
useCases.title: Node.js Migration
useCases.description: Migrate existing Node.js projects to use Bun for improved performance

useCases.title: Performance-Critical Applications
useCases.description: Build high-performance HTTP servers and APIs using Bun's native APIs

promptTemplates.title: Create New Bun Project
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the bun-development skill to help me initialize a new TypeScript project with Bun. Set up a basic project structure with testing configured.
promptTemplates.scenario: Starting a new project with Bun

promptTemplates.title: Install Dependencies
promptTemplates.prompt: Use bun-development to install the following packages: express, typescript, and @types/express. Show me how to add dev dependencies as well.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding packages to a Bun project

promptTemplates.title: Write and Run Tests
promptTemplates.prompt: Use bun-development to help me write tests for a simple math utility file. Show me how to use bun:test with common matchers.
promptTemplates.scenario: Testing with Bun's built-in test runner

promptTemplates.title: Build for Production
promptTemplates.prompt: Use bun-development to show me how to bundle my TypeScript application for production deployment, including minification and source maps.

bestPractices: Use Bun-native APIs like Bun.file() and Bun.serve() for better performance instead of Node.js compatibility layers
Keep package.json scripts simple - leverage Bun's fast execution for direct file running
Use Bun.password.hash() for secure password hashing instead of external libraries
antiPatterns: Avoid using Node.js-specific APIs like require() when import statements work in Bun
Do not rely on process.hrtime() - use Bun.nanoseconds() instead
Avoid heavy Express/Fastify setups when Bun.serve() provides faster HTTP handling

faq.question: What is Bun?
faq.answer: Bun is a fast, all-in-one JavaScript runtime designed to replace Node.js. It includes a package manager, test runner, and bundler in a single tool.

faq.question: Is Bun faster than Node.js?
faq.answer: Yes, Bun is significantly faster for startup time (about 25ms vs 100ms+), package installation (10-100x faster), and running TypeScript natively.

faq.question: Can I use existing Node.js packages with Bun?
faq.answer: Most npm packages work with Bun, but some packages with native bindings may have compatibility issues. Test your dependencies before migrating production code.

faq.question: How do I migrate from Node.js to Bun?
faq.answer: Replace npm commands with bun equivalents (npm install -> bun install), update scripts in package.json, and add @types/bun for TypeScript support.

faq.question: Does Bun support TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes, Bun has native TypeScript support. You can run .ts files directly without transpilation.

faq.question: What testing framework does Bun use?
faq.answer: Bun has a built-in test runner called bun:test that is compatible with Jest. You can use describe, it, expect, and other familiar patterns.
